page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m2.large is a burstable virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 vCPUs and 2 physical cores based on the Intel Xeon 8269CY processor, operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 4.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 0 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the lower tiers. In stress-ng testing, the single-core CPU performance scored 1087.69, while the multi-core performance reached 2161.79, categorizing both as weak or poor. Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only small block cached speed of 142.98 GB/sec, dropping to 16.56 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, and a write speed of 14.15 GB/sec. Geekbench scores further reflect this profile, with a single-core score of 891.0 and a multi-core score of 1562.0.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the burstable CPU allocation model makes this instance suitable for workloads with low baseline CPU utilization that require occasional bursts of higher performance. The lack of bundled local storage and baseline network bandwidth indicates a reliance on external network-attached storage and limits its utility for high-throughput network tasks.

Given these hardware constraints and benchmark results, the ecs.t5-c1m2.large is best suited for lightweight applications. Appropriate workloads include development and testing environments, low-traffic static web serving, basic microservices, and simple background tasks that do not require sustained high CPU or memory throughput.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m2.large is a burstable, KVM-hypervised virtual server configured with 2 vCPUs and 2 physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 4.0 GB of RAM, equivalent to 2.0 GB per core, with no bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates limited performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1087.69 and a multi-core score of 2161.79, placing it in the lower performance brackets. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, measuring 16.56 GB/sec for large uncached reads. This instance utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model, offering a cost-effective option for workloads with low baseline CPU demands. It is best suited for lightweight tasks such as development environments, low-traffic static web serving, and basic microservices.
